result.stdout:
WinSentinel — Windows Security Auditing CLI

  USAGE:
    winsentinel <command> [options]

  COMMANDS:
    --audit, -a          Run full security audit and print results
    --score, -s          Print security score and grade only
    --fix-all, -f        Run audit and auto-fix all fixable findings
    --history            View past audit runs and score trends
    --baseline <action>  Manage security baselines (save/list/check/delete)
    --checklist          Generate prioritized remediation checklist
    --profiles           List available compliance profiles
    --ignore <action>    Manage finding ignore rules (add/list/remove/clear/purge)
    --trend              Show security score trend analysis over time
    --age [action]       Finding age tracker (report/priority/chronic/new/resolved)
    --status             Quick security posture dashboard (no new scan)
    --harden             Generate reviewable PowerShell hardening script
    --policy <action>    Security Policy as Code (export/import/validate/diff)
    --threats            STRIDE threat model from audit findings
    --attack-paths       Kill chain attack path analysis with chokepoints
    --summary            Executive security summary (plain-English brief)
    --help, -h           Show this help message
    --version, -v        Show version information

  OPTIONS:
    --json, -j           Output results as JSON (machine-parseable)
    --html               Output results as HTML report
    --markdown, --md     Output results as Markdown (GitHub-flavored)
    --csv                Output results as CSV (one row per finding)
    -o, --output <file>  Save output to file instead of stdout
    --modules, -m <list> Run only specific modules (comma-separated)
    --quiet, -q          Minimal output (score + exit code only)
    --profile, -p <name> Apply compliance profile (home/developer/enterprise/server)
    --threshold, -t <n>  Exit with error if score below n (0-100)
    --compare            Compare latest two runs side-by-side (with --history)
    --diff               Show new/resolved findings between runs (with --history)
    --days <n>           History lookback period in days (default: 30)
    --limit, -l <n>      Max number of history entries to show (default: 20)
    --desc <text>        Description for a baseline (with --baseline save)
    --force              Overwrite existing baseline (with --baseline save)
    --show-ignored       Show suppressed findings in audit output
    --ignore-module <m>  Scope ignore rule to a specific module (with --ignore add)
    --ignore-severity <s> Scope ignore rule to a severity (critical/warning/info/pass)
    --ignore-reason <r>  Reason for ignoring (with --ignore add)
    --match-mode <mode>  Pattern matching: exact, contains (default), or regex
    --expire-days <n>    Auto-expire ignore rule after n days (1-3650)
    --trend-days <n>     Trend analysis lookback period in days (default: 30)
    --alert-below <n>    Alert if current score is below n (with --trend)
    --trend-modules      Include per-module trend breakdown (with --trend)
    --age-days <n>       Finding age lookback period in days (default: 90)
    --age-severity <s>   Filter age report by severity (critical/warning/info)
    --age-module <m>     Filter age report by module name
    --age-class <c>      Filter by classification (chronic/recurring/new/intermittent)
    --age-top <n>        Number of findings to show (default: 10)
    --summary-format <f> Summary format: text (default), json, md
    --summary-trend-days Trend lookback for summary (default: 30)

  PEER BENCHMARK:
    --benchmark              Compare scores against peer group benchmarks
    --benchmark-group <g>    Peer group: home, developer, enterprise, server, auto (default)
    --benchmark-format <f>   Output format: text (default), json
    --benchmark-all          Compare against all peer groups at once

  COMPLIANCE MAPPING:
    --compliance             Map findings to compliance frameworks (CIS, NIST, PCI-DSS, HIPAA)
    --compliance-framework <f>Single framework: cis, nist, pci-dss, hipaa
    --compliance-format <f>  Output format: text (default), json, markdown
    --compliance-gaps        Show only failing/partial controls (gap analysis)
    --compliance-all         Show all frameworks in detail

  SYSTEM INVENTORY:
    --inventory              Snapshot installed apps, services, ports, startup, tasks
    --inventory-format <f>   Output format: text (default), json, markdown
    --no-apps                Skip installed applications
    --no-services            Skip Windows services
    --no-ports               Skip listening ports
    --no-startup             Skip startup programs
    --no-tasks               Skip scheduled tasks

  FINDING TAG MANAGEMENT:
    --tag [action]           Manage finding tags (add/remove/list/search/report/autotag/rename/delete/export/import)
    --tag-finding <title>    Finding title to tag
    --tag-category <cat>     Finding category/module (default: Unknown)
    --tag-value <tag>        Tag value (repeatable for multiple tags)
    --tag-search <query>     Search findings by title, category, or tag
    --tag-note <text>        Add an annotation/note when tagging
    --tag-author <name>      Author for annotations
    --tag-rename-from <old>  Tag to rename (with --tag rename)
    --tag-rename-to <new>    New tag name (with --tag rename)
    --tag-file <path>        File path for tag export/import
    --tag-no-merge           Overwrite on import (default: merge)

  SECURITY FORECAST:
    --forecast               Predict future security score using historical trend analysis
    --forecast-days <n>      Forecast horizon in days (default: 30, max: 365)
    --forecast-history <n>   Days of history to analyze (default: 90)
    --forecast-format <f>    Output format: text (default), json
    --forecast-weekly        Show weekly projections instead of daily

  FINDINGS BURNDOWN:
    --burndown               Show findings burndown chart with zero-date projection
    --burndown-days <n>      Days of history to chart (default: 90, max: 365)
    --burndown-width <n>     Chart width in columns (default: 60, max: 120)
    --burndown-severity <s>  Filter: critical, high, medium, low (default: all)
    --burndown-format <f>    Output format: text (default), json

  EXAMPLES:
    winsentinel --audit                              # Full audit with colored output
    winsentinel --audit --json                       # JSON output for scripting
    winsentinel --audit --html -o report.html        # Save HTML report
    winsentinel --audit --markdown -o report.md       # Save Markdown report
    winsentinel --audit --modules firewall,network   # Scan specific modules
    winsentinel --score                              # Quick score check
    winsentinel --score --quiet                      # Score only, no formatting
    winsentinel --audit --threshold 90               # CI/CD gate: fail if < 90
    winsentinel --fix-all                            # Auto-fix all findings
    winsentinel --history                            # View past audit runs
    winsentinel --history --compare                  # Compare latest two runs
    winsentinel --history --diff                     # Show new/resolved findings
    winsentinel --history --json                     # History as JSON
    winsentinel --history --days 7                   # Last 7 days only
    winsentinel --baseline save prod                 # Save current state as baseline
    winsentinel --baseline save prod --desc "..."    # With description
    winsentinel --baseline list                      # List all saved baselines
    winsentinel --baseline check prod                # Check current vs baseline
    winsentinel --baseline check prod --json         # Check result as JSON
    winsentinel --baseline delete prod               # Delete a baseline
    winsentinel --forecast                           # 30-day security forecast
    winsentinel --forecast --forecast-days 90 --forecast-weekly  # 90-day weekly forecast
    winsentinel --forecast --json                    # Forecast as JSON
    winsentinel --checklist                          # Prioritized fix plan
    winsentinel --checklist --json                   # Checklist as JSON
    winsentinel --checklist -m firewall,network      # Checklist for specific modules
    winsentinel --profiles                           # List compliance profiles
    winsentinel --audit --profile home               # Audit with Home profile
    winsentinel --audit --profile enterprise         # Audit with Enterprise profile
    winsentinel --audit --profile server --json      # Server profile as JSON
    winsentinel --ignore add "Remote Desktop"          # Suppress findings containing text
    winsentinel --ignore add "SMB" --ignore-reason "Accepted risk"
    winsentinel --ignore add "Telemetry" --match-mode exact  # Exact title match
    winsentinel --ignore add "^BitLocker" --match-mode regex  # Regex pattern
    winsentinel --ignore add "LLMNR" --ignore-module network  # Module-scoped
    winsentinel --ignore add "audit" --ignore-severity warning  # Severity-scoped
    winsentinel --ignore add "test" --expire-days 30   # Auto-expire in 30 days
    winsentinel --ignore list                          # Show all ignore rules
    winsentinel --ignore remove abc12345               # Remove rule by ID
    winsentinel --ignore clear                         # Remove all rules
    winsentinel --ignore purge                         # Remove expired rules
    winsentinel --audit --show-ignored                 # Audit showing suppressed findings
    winsentinel --trend                                # Score trend analysis
    winsentinel --trend --trend-days 90                # 90-day trend window
    winsentinel --trend --alert-below 80               # Alert if score < 80
    winsentinel --trend --trend-modules                # Include per-module breakdown
    winsentinel --trend --json                         # Trend data as JSON
    winsentinel --age                                   # Full finding age report
    winsentinel --age priority                           # Priority queue (fix first)
    winsentinel --age chronic                            # Show chronic findings
    winsentinel --age new                                # Show new findings
    winsentinel --age resolved                           # Show resolved findings
    winsentinel --age --age-severity critical            # Critical findings only
    winsentinel --age --age-module firewall              # Filter by module
    winsentinel --age --age-days 7 --json               # Last 7 days as JSON

  NOISE ANALYSIS:
    --noise                  Identify noisiest finding sources across audit history
    --noise-days <n>         Analysis window in days (default: 90)
    --noise-top <n>          Number of top noisy items to show (default: 15)
    --noise-format <fmt>     Output format: text, json, markdown
    winsentinel --noise                                 # Full noise analysis
    winsentinel --noise --noise-days 30 --noise-top 5   # Last 30 days, top 5
    winsentinel --noise --json                          # Noise data as JSON

  CALENDAR HEATMAP:
    --heatmap                Show GitHub-style calendar heatmap of audit activity
    --heatmap-weeks <n>      Number of weeks to display (default: 26)
    --heatmap-format <fmt>   Output format: text, json, markdown
    winsentinel --heatmap                               # 6-month activity heatmap
    winsentinel --heatmap --heatmap-weeks 52            # Full year heatmap
    winsentinel --heatmap --json                        # Heatmap data as JSON

  EXIT CODES:
    0  All checks pass (or score >= threshold)
    1  Warnings found (or score < threshold)
    2  Critical findings found (or trend alert triggered)
    3  Error during execution

  AVAILABLE MODULES:
    firewall, updates, defender, accounts, network,
    processes, startup, system, privacy, browser,
    appsecurity, encryption, eventlog

commands.payload:
WinSentinel — Windows Security Auditing CLI

  USAGE:
    winsentinel <command> [options]

  COMMANDS:
    --audit, -a          Run full security audit and print results
    --score, -s          Print security score and grade only
    --fix-all, -f        Run audit and auto-fix all fixable findings
    --history            View past audit runs and score trends
    --baseline <action>  Manage security baselines (save/list/check/delete)
    --checklist          Generate prioritized remediation checklist
    --profiles           List available compliance profiles
    --ignore <action>    Manage finding ignore rules (add/list/remove/clear/purge)
    --trend              Show security score trend analysis over time
    --age [action]       Finding age tracker (report/priority/chronic/new/resolved)
    --status             Quick security posture dashboard (no new scan)
    --harden             Generate reviewable PowerShell hardening script
    --policy <action>    Security Policy as Code (export/import/validate/diff)
    --threats            STRIDE threat model from audit findings
    --attack-paths       Kill chain attack path analysis with chokepoints
    --summary            Executive security summary (plain-English brief)
    --help, -h           Show this help message
    --version, -v        Show version information

  OPTIONS:
    --json, -j           Output results as JSON (machine-parseable)
    --html               Output results as HTML report
    --markdown, --md     Output results as Markdown (GitHub-flavored)
    --csv                Output results as CSV (one row per finding)
    -o, --output <file>  Save output to file instead of stdout
    --modules, -m <list> Run only specific modules (comma-separated)
    --quiet, -q          Minimal output (score + exit code only)
    --profile, -p <name> Apply compliance profile (home/developer/enterprise/server)
    --threshold, -t <n>  Exit with error if score below n (0-100)
    --compare            Compare latest two runs side-by-side (with --history)
    --diff               Show new/resolved findings between runs (with --history)
    --days <n>           History lookback period in days (default: 30)
    --limit, -l <n>      Max number of history entries to show (default: 20)
    --desc <text>        Description for a baseline (with --baseline save)
    --force              Overwrite existing baseline (with --baseline save)
    --show-ignored       Show suppressed findings in audit output
    --ignore-module <m>  Scope ignore rule to a specific module (with --ignore add)
    --ignore-severity <s> Scope ignore rule to a severity (critical/warning/info/pass)
    --ignore-reason <r>  Reason for ignoring (with --ignore add)
    --match-mode <mode>  Pattern matching: exact, contains (default), or regex
    --expire-days <n>    Auto-expire ignore rule after n days (1-3650)
    --trend-days <n>     Trend analysis lookback period in days (default: 30)
    --alert-below <n>    Alert if current score is below n (with --trend)
    --trend-modules      Include per-module trend breakdown (with --trend)
    --age-days <n>       Finding age lookback period in days (default: 90)
    --age-severity <s>   Filter age report by severity (critical/warning/info)
    --age-module <m>     Filter age report by module name
    --age-class <c>      Filter by classification (chronic/recurring/new/intermittent)
    --age-top <n>        Number of findings to show (default: 10)
    --summary-format <f> Summary format: text (default), json, md
    --summary-trend-days Trend lookback for summary (default: 30)

  PEER BENCHMARK:
    --benchmark              Compare scores against peer group benchmarks
    --benchmark-group <g>    Peer group: home, developer, enterprise, server, auto (default)
    --benchmark-format <f>   Output format: text (default), json
    --benchmark-all          Compare against all peer groups at once

  COMPLIANCE MAPPING:
    --compliance             Map findings to compliance frameworks (CIS, NIST, PCI-DSS, HIPAA)
    --compliance-framework <f>Single framework: cis, nist, pci-dss, hipaa
    --compliance-format <f>  Output format: text (default), json, markdown
    --compliance-gaps        Show only failing/partial controls (gap analysis)
    --compliance-all         Show all frameworks in detail

  SYSTEM INVENTORY:
    --inventory              Snapshot installed apps, services, ports, startup, tasks
    --inventory-format <f>   Output format: text (default), json, markdown
    --no-apps                Skip installed applications
    --no-services            Skip Windows services
    --no-ports               Skip listening ports
    --no-startup             Skip startup programs
    --no-tasks               Skip scheduled tasks

  FINDING TAG MANAGEMENT:
    --tag [action]           Manage finding tags (add/remove/list/search/report/autotag/rename/delete/export/import)
    --tag-finding <title>    Finding title to tag
    --tag-category <cat>     Finding category/module (default: Unknown)
    --tag-value <tag>        Tag value (repeatable for multiple tags)
    --tag-search <query>     Search findings by title, category, or tag
    --tag-note <text>        Add an annotation/note when tagging
    --tag-author <name>      Author for annotations
    --tag-rename-from <old>  Tag to rename (with --tag rename)
    --tag-rename-to <new>    New tag name (with --tag rename)
    --tag-file <path>        File path for tag export/import
    --tag-no-merge           Overwrite on import (default: merge)

  SECURITY FORECAST:
    --forecast               Predict future security score using historical trend analysis
    --forecast-days <n>      Forecast horizon in days (default: 30, max: 365)
    --forecast-history <n>   Days of history to analyze (default: 90)
    --forecast-format <f>    Output format: text (default), json
    --forecast-weekly        Show weekly projections instead of daily

  FINDINGS BURNDOWN:
    --burndown               Show findings burndown chart with zero-date projection
    --burndown-days <n>      Days of history to chart (default: 90, max: 365)
    --burndown-width <n>     Chart width in columns (default: 60, max: 120)
    --burndown-severity <s>  Filter: critical, high, medium, low (default: all)
    --burndown-format <f>    Output format: text (default), json

  EXAMPLES:
    winsentinel --audit                              # Full audit with colored output
    winsentinel --audit --json                       # JSON output for scripting
    winsentinel --audit --html -o report.html        # Save HTML report
    winsentinel --audit --markdown -o report.md       # Save Markdown report
    winsentinel --audit --modules firewall,network   # Scan specific modules
    winsentinel --score                              # Quick score check
    winsentinel --score --quiet                      # Score only, no formatting
    winsentinel --audit --threshold 90               # CI/CD gate: fail if < 90
    winsentinel --fix-all                            # Auto-fix all findings
    winsentinel --history                            # View past audit runs
    winsentinel --history --compare                  # Compare latest two runs
    winsentinel --history --diff                     # Show new/resolved findings
    winsentinel --history --json                     # History as JSON
    winsentinel --history --days 7                   # Last 7 days only
    winsentinel --baseline save prod                 # Save current state as baseline
    winsentinel --baseline save prod --desc "..."    # With description
    winsentinel --baseline list                      # List all saved baselines
    winsentinel --baseline check prod                # Check current vs baseline
    winsentinel --baseline check prod --json         # Check result as JSON
    winsentinel --baseline delete prod               # Delete a baseline
    winsentinel --forecast                           # 30-day security forecast
    winsentinel --forecast --forecast-days 90 --forecast-weekly  # 90-day weekly forecast
    winsentinel --forecast --json                    # Forecast as JSON
    winsentinel --checklist                          # Prioritized fix plan
    winsentinel --checklist --json                   # Checklist as JSON
    winsentinel --checklist -m firewall,network      # Checklist for specific modules
    winsentinel --profiles                           # List compliance profiles
    winsentinel --audit --profile home               # Audit with Home profile
    winsentinel --audit --profile enterprise         # Audit with Enterprise profile
    winsentinel --audit --profile server --json      # Server profile as JSON
    winsentinel --ignore add "Remote Desktop"          # Suppress findings containing text
    winsentinel --ignore add "SMB" --ignore-reason "Accepted risk"
    winsentinel --ignore add "Telemetry" --match-mode exact  # Exact title match
    winsentinel --ignore add "^BitLocker" --match-mode regex  # Regex pattern
    winsentinel --ignore add "LLMNR" --ignore-module network  # Module-scoped
    winsentinel --ignore add "audit" --ignore-severity warning  # Severity-scoped
    winsentinel --ignore add "test" --expire-days 30   # Auto-expire in 30 days
    winsentinel --ignore list                          # Show all ignore rules
    winsentinel --ignore remove abc12345               # Remove rule by ID
    winsentinel --ignore clear                         # Remove all rules
    winsentinel --ignore purge                         # Remove expired rules
    winsentinel --audit --show-ignored                 # Audit showing suppressed findings
    winsentinel --trend                                # Score trend analysis
    winsentinel --trend --trend-days 90                # 90-day trend window
    winsentinel --trend --alert-below 80               # Alert if score < 80
    winsentinel --trend --trend-modules                # Include per-module breakdown
    winsentinel --trend --json                         # Trend data as JSON
    winsentinel --age                                   # Full finding age report
    winsentinel --age priority                           # Priority queue (fix first)
    winsentinel --age chronic                            # Show chronic findings
    winsentinel --age new                                # Show new findings
    winsentinel --age resolved                           # Show resolved findings
    winsentinel --age --age-severity critical            # Critical findings only
    winsentinel --age --age-module firewall              # Filter by module
    winsentinel --age --age-days 7 --json               # Last 7 days as JSON

  NOISE ANALYSIS:
    --noise                  Identify noisiest finding sources across audit history
    --noise-days <n>         Analysis window in days (default: 90)
    --noise-top <n>          Number of top noisy items to show (default: 15)
    --noise-format <fmt>     Output format: text, json, markdown
    winsentinel --noise                                 # Full noise analysis
    winsentinel --noise --noise-days 30 --noise-top 5   # Last 30 days, top 5
    winsentinel --noise --json                          # Noise data as JSON

  CALENDAR HEATMAP:
    --heatmap                Show GitHub-style calendar heatmap of audit activity
    --heatmap-weeks <n>      Number of weeks to display (default: 26)
    --heatmap-format <fmt>   Output format: text, json, markdown
    winsentinel --heatmap                               # 6-month activity heatmap
    winsentinel --heatmap --heatmap-weeks 52            # Full year heatmap
    winsentinel --heatmap --json                        # Heatmap data as JSON

  EXIT CODES:
    0  All checks pass (or score >= threshold)
    1  Warnings found (or score < threshold)
    2  Critical findings found (or trend alert triggered)
    3  Error during execution

  AVAILABLE MODULES:
    firewall, updates, defender, accounts, network,
    processes, startup, system, privacy, browser,
    appsecurity, encryption, eventlog
